GILGAMESHARCHER-OATH_OF_URUK.title: Oath of the King of Uruk
GILGAMESHARCHER-OATH_OF_URUK.description: #Bond: earn points from victories, events, shops and rest sites. Each level grants permanent gifts (Max HP, starting NP Charge, starting Block). At Bond 7 you start each combat with 20 NP Charge. At Bond 10 you start each combat with 1 Critical Hit ready.
GILGAMESHARCHER-OATH_OF_URUK.flavor: The covenant between the King and the city he was born to rule. While the bond holds, the King of Heroes wields his treasures at full weight.
GILGAMESHARCHER-CATALOG_OF_THE_ROYAL_TRE: #At card rewards, give up the card to roll for a dupe. A dupe raises your NP Level (up to 5), boosting your Noble Phantasm cards by 15% per level.
Every treasure ever forged, indexed by the King's own hand. He owns the originals; everything since is a copy.
GILGAMESHARCHER-KINGS_WINE_CUP.title: King's Wine Cup
GILGAMESHARCHER-KINGS_WINE_CUP.descripti: #At the end of each combat, gain !Gold! Gold.
GILGAMESHARCHER-KINGS_WINE_CUP.flavor: The King drinks, and the treasury fills. Wealth flows to him the way rivers flow to the sea.
GILGAMESHARCHER-MANTLE_OF_ARROGANCE.desc: #Whenever one of your attacks kills an enemy while you have *Critical *Ready, gain !Gold! Gold (up to 15 per combat).
GILGAMESHARCHER-MANTLE_OF_ARROGANCE.flav: He does not loot the dead. The dead simply surrender their gold to the one who was always its rightful owner.
GILGAMESHARCHER-VIMANA_GOLDEN_THRONE.des: #The first time you play 2 or more Treasure Arms in a single turn each combat, draw !Cards!.
GILGAMESHARCHER-VIMANA_GOLDEN_THRONE.fla: The flying throne of the King. Why march to war when the war can be made to come to you?
#The first *Weak you would receive each combat is negated.
Rank E Magic Resistance: only the pettiest sorcery slides off. A king does not deign to dodge the rest.
GILGAMESHARCHER-THE_ORIGINAL_CHALICE.des: #Gain !MaxHp! Max HP. While held, limits break: Bond can reach level 12 and NP Level can reach 6.
GILGAMESHARCHER-THE_ORIGINAL_CHALICE.fla: A Holy Grail? Mongrel. I have thousands of them in my vault — this is merely the first.
